name: DeepSeek-V4
description: DeepSeek's preview flagship family: V4-Pro (1.6T total / 49B active MoE, the largest open-weight release ever) and V4-Flash (284B/13B). 1M-token context with up to 384K output, built on manifold-constrained Hyper Connections and Constrained Sparse Attention. MIT license. Vendor benchmark claims await broad independent verification.

evidence.value: Vendor reports state-of-the-art open-model coding results for V4-Pro, exceeding V3.2
V4 release (2026-04-24) documented as DeepSeek's strongest coding model; independent replication still in progress

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Vendor-reported coding benchmarks; medium confidence pending broad independent verification of preview-release claims

evidence.value: Vendor claims frontier reasoning performance, building on V3.2-Speciale's olympiad-level results

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Vendor-reported reasoning benchmarks; medium confidence until third-party evaluations of the preview mature

evidence.value: Early community evaluations place V4-Pro at or near the top of open-model leaderboards

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Community leaderboard positions and vendor benchmarks for a preview release

evidence.value: Preview status: behavior may change before stable release; community reports occasional long-output instability near the 384K limit

output_consistency.methodology: Repeated-prompt testing and community preview feedback

evidence.value: V4-Pro median latency ~2.2s; V4-Flash sub-second for standard prompts

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes from independent benchmarking

evidence.value: p95 ~5.5s for V4-Pro; long-context and long-output requests substantially longer

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads from independent benchmarking

evidence.value: 1M-token context window with up to 384K output tokens, enabled by Constrained Sparse Attention
Post-launch demand spikes caused intermittent degradation in late April/May 2026; stabilizing since

performance_reliability.notes: Largest open-weight release ever (V4-Pro: 1.6T total / 49B active). Vendor benchmarks are impressive but this is a preview: score confidence is medium until independent verification matures. V4-Flash (284B/13B) offers a much cheaper deployment point.

evidence.value: Comparable to V3.2 on common injection patterns; preview red-teaming coverage still thin

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection patterns; limited preview-stage coverage

evidence.value: Standard alignment guardrails; open weights mean alignment is removable downstream

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Adversarial prompt testing; assessment accounts for open-weight modifiability

evidence.value: Standard first-party API data handling; self-hosting gives complete data control

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy plus self-hosting option for full data isolation

evidence.value: Safety post-training applied; preview-stage safety evaluation less complete than for stable releases

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories on default weights

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS-only, rate limiting on the first-party platform

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and transport guarantees

security.notes: Security posture mirrors V3.2 but with thinner preview-stage red-team coverage. Open weights shift safety responsibility to deployers who fine-tune or self-host.

data_residency.value: China (first-party API); anywhere via self-hosting or third-party hosts

evidence.value: First-party API data processed and stored in China; MIT-licensed weights allow deployment in any jurisdiction

data_residency.methodology: Review of privacy policy and hosting options; China-jurisdiction caveat applies only to the first-party API

evidence.value: API data usage terms documented; self-hosting removes the concern entirely

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms for the hosted API

data_retention.value: Per first-party API policy (China-hosted); zero when self-hosted

evidence.value: First-party API retention follows Chinese regulatory requirements; self-hosted deployments retain nothing externally

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service; retention is deployment-dependent for open-weight models

evidence.value: No built-in PII redaction tooling; customer responsible on any deployment

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: No SOC 2, HIPAA, or FedRAMP on the first-party API; compliant deployments achievable via certified Western hosts or self-hosting

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of certifications for the first-party platform; third-party hosts inherit their own certifications

evidence.value: No zero-retention option on the first-party API; self-hosting provides true zero external retention

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of data handling across first-party API, third-party hosts, and self-hosting

privacy_compliance.notes: Same split as all DeepSeek releases: the first-party API is China-hosted with China-jurisdiction residency and no Western certifications, while self-hosting or Western third-party hosting avoids those concerns. V4-Pro's 1.6T size makes self-hosting far harder than V4-Flash.

evidence.value: Visible reasoning traces; up to 384K output supports very long inspectable chains of thought

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and trace accessibility

evidence.value: Preliminary results suggest parity with or improvement over V3.2; sample sizes still small for the preview

hallucination_rate.methodology: Limited factual QA testing during the preview period

evidence.value: Topic-avoidance on politically sensitive subjects persists from prior releases; formal bias audits of V4 not yet published

bias_fairness.methodology: Preliminary bias probing; formal evaluations pending

evidence.value: Expresses uncertainty in reasoning traces; final-answer calibration unverified for the preview

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Release notes document architecture (Hyper Connections, Constrained Sparse Attention) and pricing; full technical report expected with the stable release

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of preview documentation completeness against DeepSeek's historical technical-report standard

evidence.value: High-level methodology described; dataset composition not disclosed in detail

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Standard alignment guardrails in released weights; preview safety evaluation ongoing

guardrails.methodology: Analysis of built-in safety mechanisms in default weights

trust_transparency.notes: Architectural novelty (manifold-constrained Hyper Connections, Constrained Sparse Attention) is disclosed, but the preview lacks the full technical report and independent benchmark replication DeepSeek usually delivers. Treat vendor claims with medium confidence.

evidence.value: OpenAI-compatible API; V4-Pro and V4-Flash endpoints with context caching ($0.0028/1M cache hits on Flash)

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: OpenAI-SDK compatibility; vLLM and SGLang support landed for V4 architecture within weeks of release

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK compatibility and inference-framework support

evidence.value: Legacy deepseek-chat/deepseek-reasoner endpoints deprecate 2026-07-24, a three-month migration window

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of deprecation timelines and migration windows

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with token metrics; full observability when self-hosting

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of monitoring tools across deployment options

evidence.value: Community and email support only; no enterprise SLA; preview status adds change risk

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Third-party hosts onboarding rapidly; V4-Pro's 1.6T footprint limits the number of providers able to serve it, while V4-Flash adoption is broad

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party hosting availability six weeks post-release

evidence.value: MIT license: unrestricted commercial use, modification, and redistribution

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Aggressive pricing (V4-Pro $0.435/$0.87, V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28 per 1M) and MIT licensing, but the short legacy-endpoint deprecation window (2026-07-24) and preview status demand migration agility. V4-Pro self-hosting is feasible only for well-resourced organizations.

code-generation.notes: Vendor-reported state-of-the-art open-model coding; 1M context fits entire large repositories. Preview status warrants validation on your own tasks.

customer-support.notes: V4-Flash is a strong cheap option for high-volume support with aggressive cache-hit pricing.

content-creation.notes: Up to 384K output enables book-length single-pass drafts; prose quality solid but not best-in-class.

data-analysis.notes: 1M context plus strong reasoning makes whole-dataset and multi-document analysis practical at open-model prices.

research-assistant.notes: 1M-token context ingests entire literature corpora; frontier reasoning lineage from V3.2-Speciale.

legal-compliance.notes: China-hosted first-party API and preview status are both disqualifying for most regulated legal work; self-hosting V4-Flash is the viable path.

healthcare.notes: No HIPAA path on the first-party API; preview status adds change risk. Only self-hosted compliant deployments are viable.

financial-analysis.notes: Excellent quantitative reasoning over very long filings at low cost; verify vendor claims and plan data residency for regulated use.

education.notes: V4-Flash pricing is ideal for education-scale deployment with strong math reasoning.

creative-writing.notes: Massive output length helps novel-scale drafting; stylistic range remains behind dedicated creative leaders.

strengths: Largest open-weight release ever: V4-Pro at 1.6T total / 49B active parameters under MIT license
1M-token context window with up to 384K output tokens
Novel architecture: manifold-constrained Hyper Connections and Constrained Sparse Attention
Aggressive pricing: V4-Pro $0.435/$0.87 and V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28 per 1M tokens ($0.0028 cache hits on Flash)
V4-Flash (284B/13B) offers a practical self-hosting and high-volume deployment point
Inherits DeepSeek's frontier reasoning lineage from V3.2/Speciale
limitations: Preview status: behavior, pricing, and endpoints may change; vendor benchmark claims not yet broadly independently verified
First-party API is China-hosted: China-jurisdiction data residency and no SOC 2/HIPAA/FedRAMP (self-hosting or Western hosts avoid this)
V4-Pro's 1.6T footprint makes self-hosting impractical for all but the largest organizations
Short migration window: legacy deepseek-chat/deepseek-reasoner endpoints deprecate 2026-07-24
Text-only: no native vision or audio
No enterprise SLA or dedicated support on the first-party platform
Full technical report not yet published for the preview
best_for: Million-token context workloads: whole-repository coding, corpus-scale research, multi-document analysis
Cost-sensitive high-volume inference via V4-Flash with cache-hit pricing
Organizations wanting the most capable MIT-licensed weights available
Teams already on DeepSeek migrating off the deprecating legacy endpoints
not_recommended_for: Production systems that cannot tolerate preview-stage changes
Regulated Western workloads via the first-party API
Multimodal applications requiring vision or audio
Self-hosting V4-Pro without large-scale multi-node GPU infrastructure

pricing.notes: Preview pricing per official pricing page; legacy deepseek-chat/deepseek-reasoner endpoints deprecate 2026-07-24. Self-hosting is infrastructure-cost-only under MIT license.

metadata.architecture: Mixture-of-Experts with manifold-constrained Hyper Connections and Constrained Sparse Attention; V4-Pro 1.6T total / 49B active, V4-Flash 284B total / 13B active
metadata.parameters: V4-Pro: 1.6T total / 49B active; V4-Flash: 284B total / 13B active
